topic.what: Lightweight protocol-agnostic security layer using backscatter communication for SWIPT-enabled battery-free IoT nodes
topic.how: Uses Backscattering Rectifier to modulate identification signals onto wireless power waveform for authentication without activating RF transceivers
topic.why_matters: Addresses security vulnerabilities in energy-constrained IoT devices (replay attacks on ABP encryption) while maintaining energy efficiency

characteristics.what_it_measures: RF-to-DC conversion efficiency of the Backscattering Rectifier
characteristics.evidence: Fig. 7b shows >20% efficiency across typical input power levels at 868 MHz ISM band
characteristics.context: Measured across -20 to +10 dBm input range at 868 MHz ISM band

characteristics.what_it_measures: DC power output at cold-start input level
characteristics.evidence: Achieves 15 µW at -13 dBm RF input, sufficient for BQ25504 PMU cold-start
characteristics.context: Cold-start operation threshold for BFSN power management unit

characteristics.what_it_measures: Signal-to-noise ratio of backscattered identification signal
characteristics.evidence: Fig. 9 shows 16.6 dB dynamic range wired; Fig. 13 and 15 show ~1 dB and ~0.5 dB wireless
characteristics.context: Wired setup uses circulator isolation; wireless setup affected by cross-jamming and environmental reflections

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time required for backscattering identification sequence
characteristics.evidence: Fig. 11 shows 2 ms backscattering contribution in BFSN operation cycle
characteristics.context: Transmits 16-byte Private Key Manchester-encoded at up to 100 kHz modulation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reflection coefficient showing impedance matching quality
characteristics.evidence: Fig. 6a shows S11 below -10 dB within 868 MHz ISM band at typical input levels
characteristics.context: Effective matching maintained despite diode nonlinearity across frequency range

characteristics.evidence: Stable modulation with clear dynamic range achieved up to 100 kHz
characteristics.context: Demonstrates feasibility for high-speed backscattering applications
